title: Time zero behavior varies on tracing
body: ### Steps to reproduce **Version**: Verilator 4.104 2020-11-14 rev v4.102-88-g02cb3d17a Files for reproduction: [repro.tar.gz](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/files/5625211/repro.tar.gz) 1. Untar the file: ``` tar xvf repro.tar.gz ``` 2. Run the build script (just runs verilator and executes the binary) ``` ./gen-vcd out.sv ``` 3. This generates a vcd file (`out.vcd`) and writes the following to STDOUT: ``` Simulated 4 cycles [mem]: 0 ``` 4. Check the value of `main.Res0_0.mem[0][0]` in your favorite waveform viewer. It will be `0`. **Expected output**: Running the simulation writes ``` Simulated 4 cycles [mem]: 20 ``` and the value in `main.Res0_0.mem[0][0]` contains the value `20`. ### Investigation The program implemented in `out.sv` exponentiates a value and writes it to a memory. The memory "primitive" is implemented as: ```verilog module std_mem_d2 #(parameter width = 32, parameter d0_size = 16, parameter d1_size = 16, parameter d0_idx_size = 4, parameter d1_idx_size = 4) (input logic [d0_idx_size-1:0] addr0, input logic [d1_idx_size-1:0] addr1, input logic [width-1:0] write_data, input logic write_en, input logic clk, output logic [width-1:0] read_data, output logic done); /* verilator lint_off WIDTH */ logic [width-1:0] mem[d0_size-1:0][d1_size-1:0]; assign read_data = mem[addr0][addr1]; always_ff @(posedge clk) begin if (write_en) begin mem[addr0][addr1] <= write_data; done <= 1'd1; end else done <= 1'd0; end endmodule ``` During normal execution of the file, the `write_data` and `write_en` signals are held high for a cycle: However, the memory (in the image `mem(0)(0)`) never shows the values. What's weird is that the following changes all "fix" the problems: 1. Add `/* verilator public */` to `write_data`. 2. Add an unused register to `std_mem_d2` and write the `write_data` signal into it. 3. Add `/* verilator public */` to `mem`. This only makes the value appear on STDOUT and no the VCD. I don't really understand why any of these work so I want to figure out what is going on here. My guess is that somewhere, the `write_data` signal get optimized away. ### Context This code is generated by a HLS-like [compiler](https://github.com/cucapra/futil/). If there is some issue with us misusing `always_ff`, `logic` etc., please let us know.
